text: Welcome to the show. Today we're talking about artificial intelligence and the future of technology.
I think one of the most counterintuitive things about AI is that the models that seem the most impressive today might not be the ones that matter most in five years.
The real breakthrough will come from systems that can reason, not just pattern match.
Most people assume that scaling is the only path forward, but that's actually a controversial take in the research community.
Some of the best researchers I know believe we're hitting fundamental limits.
Here's a story about a conversation I had with a researcher at a major lab.
She told me that the internal results they weren't publishing were actually more surprising than what they were showing the public.
That's the kind of vulnerable honesty you rarely get in this industry.
My prediction for 2025 is that we'll see the first truly agentic systems deployed in production at scale, not demos, actual production systems.
And that's going to change everything about how we think about software engineering as a profession.
The funny thing is, most software engineers I talk to are not worried at all.
They think AI is just a better autocomplete.
That's hilarious and also kind of terrifying.
